claim: Creating a CLI tool for agents to edit eval YAML files significantly improves agent ability to work with large eval suites by avoiding context limits
actionable: Build a small CLI (like eval_tool) that lets agents query, edit, and add test cases without including full YAML in context to prevent hitting context window limits.
source_title: Fighting AI with AI — Lawrence Jones, Incident

claim: Serializing UI debugging views as downloadable file systems enables agents to trace prompt hierarchies and identify exact prompts needing changes
actionable: Export your UI interactions, traces, and debugging views as text-based file systems (not HTML) and drop them into Claude Code sessions so agents can trace through prompt hierarchies.
source_title: Fighting AI with AI — Lawrence Jones, Incident

claim: Running parallel agents analyzing individual investigations, then clustering results surfaces systemic failure patterns across customer accounts
actionable: Use a two-stage pipeline: spawn 25+ agents in parallel each analyzing one investigation, then use a secondary stage to cluster and identify meta-patterns across results.
source_title: Fighting AI with AI — Lawrence Jones, Incident

claim: File systems are exceptionally good agent context compared to MCPs or HTML—agents can grep through bulk downloaded data more effectively than querying APIs
actionable: Prefer downloading all needed information as a file system for agent context rather than relying on MCPs or API calls—agents perform better with grep-able bulk data.
source_title: Fighting AI with AI — Lawrence Jones, Incident

claim: Complex AI systems require their own internal debugging tools tuned for agent use, not just human-optimized UIs
actionable: When building AI systems, prioritize debugging tools that agents can effectively use (file systems, CLIs) alongside or before human-readable UIs.
source_title: Fighting AI with AI — Lawrence Jones, Incident
